task_yaml.task:
I have this cropped close-up of a famous historic photograph, with no caption
or credit, and I want to know exactly what it depicts and find the original
archival record from the source institution (not a Wikipedia or news copy).
The image is here:

    /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/crop.jpg

It's only a detail of the full photo — no caption, no ID text — so identify it
from the image itself and then trace it back to the authoritative archive.

Tell me:
- what the photograph depicts (event/subject and people if identifiable),
- the date it was taken,
- the holding archive and the canonical record (the archive's own
  page / image-library id), and the photo's archive id,
- the photographer if known.

Save your conclusion and the supporting evidence (how you identified it, and
the canonical archive record URL) to
`/tmp_workspace/results/photo_provenance.json`, plus notes/screenshots under
`/tmp_workspace/results/`.
